Describe the sequence of physical actions performed on the casserole dish from the beginning to the end of the clip.
canonical_answer: At 0s, a hand moves over the casserole dish in a metal pan; at 12s, a fork begins pressing texture into the mashed potatoes; at 14s, the ridged pattern becomes distinct on the surface; at 18s, the blue-handled fork rests on the food; finally at 20s, scissors with black handles appear on the surface.

question: Track the visible kitchen tools and their interaction with the food from start to finish.
canonical_answer: At 0s, a metal box grater with a red handle sits in the corner; at 12s, a metal fork presses into the potato topping; at 14s, the fork creates a cross-hatch pattern on the food; at 18s, the blue-handled fork stops moving and rests; at 20s, scissors with black handles appear near the tray.
